overall_reasoning: The patch delivers the requested composition and nullable completion support with semantics that were self-reviewed and corrected during the trajectory (allOf intersection, oneOf overlap exclusion, alternative-branch requiredness, pure-null snippet). The oneOf value heuristic can still over-suggest when branches only impose non-enumerable constraints (e.g. bare type: string), and stale-cache existing-key filtering can theoretically use a mismatched array element after structural edits, but these are acceptable completion heuristics rather than correctness blockers. Focused lib, completion, and hover suites plus clippy and fmt all pass; the only full-suite failure is an unrelated network-dependent snapshot, which is not attributable to this change. The change is scoped, tested, and committed as instructed.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: New helpers (merge_property, merge_schema_type, resolve_completion_subschemas, value_suggestion_values, suggestions_from_values) are sensibly decomposed and documented, but schema.rs accumulates a fair amount of intricate set-merging logic whose 'empty means unconstrained' heuristic is only implicit in code. Snippet type preference is clear and restores the null arm.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: Reuses existing primitives (follow_ref, MAX_SCHEMA_DEPTH, format_annotation) and the established in-process LSP test harness; follows existing naming/comment style; formatted, clippy-clean, and committed per the task instruction.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: Changes are confined to the completion pipeline (parse context, schema collection, item building) plus tests/fixtures. Hover resolution and unrelated schema behavior are deliberately untouched, and the generated .snap.new artifact was removed before committing only the five intended files.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: Retains cycle detection and depth caps on all new recursive traversals, no unsafe code, no new network or filesystem behavior; dedup prevents unbounded duplicate items from adversarial composition.
rating_rationales.test_quality: Strong coverage: parse-level tests for array index pointers and direct-array None; schema unit tests for metadata merging, nullable formatting, allOf intersection, and oneOf overlap exclusion; protocol-level LSP tests against the real config.schema.json with exact-label and uniqueness assertions, including existing-key filtering inside arrays.

strengths: Completion scanner now tracks array indices so objects nested in arrays get correct JSON pointers, and returns None for cursors directly inside arrays as documented
Composition-aware collection: allOf value candidates intersect, anyOf unions, oneOf excludes values accepted by multiple branches, with unit tests pinning each semantic
Nullable type arrays (e.g. ["boolean", "null"]) handled for details, snippets, and value suggestions; pure-null snippet behavior preserved via fallback
Duplicate suggestions prevented at both the schema-merge layer and the final completion-item layer
Realistic LSP integration tests exercise the repository's own config.schema.json chain (items -> $ref -> anyOf -> allOf -> $ref) and assert uniqueness
Hover path (resolve_subschema / lookup_hover_content) untouched; hover suite passes; work committed as a single focused commit
summary: Implements composed-schema (allOf/anyOf/oneOf) and nullable-type completions with correct merge/dedup semantics, fixes array-element pointer tracking, adds realistic LSP and unit tests, preserves hover behavior, and is committed cleanly.
